thesis_card.headline: NVDA: AI/Accelerated Computing Dominance Drives Exceptional Growth and Margin Expansion
thesis_card.why_now: NVDA is executing at an extraordinary pace with Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY) and gross margin expanding to 74.9% from 60.5%, driven by Blackwell platform ramp and non-recurrence of prior-year H20 charges. Data Center networking revenue accelerated 142% YoY as customers deploy NVLink and Ethernet/InfiniBand fabrics alongside compute, signaling sustained multi-year AI infrastructure cycle. Compute Networking operating income grew 142% to $53.3B, demonstrating operating leverage. Management's 25x dividend increase (to $0.25/share in May 2026) signals confidence in durable cash generation.
thesis_card.expected_edge: NVDA's integrated compute-networking platform creates structural competitive moat as customers increasingly require fabric solutions alongside accelerators. Blackwell platform adoption remains early-cycle with sequential edge revenue growth of 10% QoQ suggesting demand remains robust. At 74.9% gross margins, the company has pricing power and optionality to invest in future platforms while returning capital. Five-year stock performance (+1,348% vs S&P 500's +100%) demonstrates ability to create shareholder value despite increasingly larger scale.
thesis_card.risks: Valuation risk if growth deceleration or margin compression occurs as the law of large numbers applies to a $215.9B revenue base. Competitive risk from AMD/Intel in both compute and networking, though currently limited. Customer concentration in hyperscalers creates execution risk if any major customer pulls forward demand. Regulatory/geopolitical risks around export controls to China could constrain TAM. Inventory channel dynamics and potential demand normalization post-Blackwell cycle ramp warrant monitoring.

key_evidence.quote: Exceptional revenue growth of 65% year-over-year for fiscal 2026, with total revenue of $215.9 billion versus $130.5 billion in fiscal 2025, driven primarily by accelerated computing and AI solutions in the Data Center segment
Q1 FY2027 total revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, with strong gross margin improvement to 74.9% from 60.5% a year ago
Data Center computing revenue grew 59% driven by demand for Blackwell computing platform, while Data Center networking grew 142% driven by NVLink compute fabric and Ethernet/InfiniBand platforms

Compute Networking segment operating income grew 142% year-over-year in Q1 FY2027 to $53.3 billion from $22.1 billion, driven by strong revenue growth and the one-year prior non-recurrence of the H20 charge
Dividend increased 25x from $0.01 to $0.25 per share on May 18, 2026, reflecting management confidence in the company's cash generation and financial strength
